financingNotes: Corporate financing bar reflects estimated debt principal amortization (~7-year) plus cash interest on ~US$4.3B net debt (Q4 2025 balance sheet), shown as cash outflows. Override via balanceDebt on company page when market data is loaded.

assets.name: Côté Gold
assets.notes: 2026 guidance: 270,000-310,000 oz (70% attributable); 390,000-440,000 oz (100% basis). AISC midpoint $1,850/oz. Production profile ramps through Years 2-5 (non-flat) to reflect Cote commissioning/ramp-up trajectory in corporate presentations. Capex is front-loaded and tapers after Year1 as major ramp-related spend declines. Currency: USD for AISC; capex based on disclosed guidance context.

assets.name: Essakane
assets.notes: 2026 guidance midpoint 360 koz (85% attributable) and AISC midpoint $2,075/oz. Years 2-5 modeled with non-flat profile and gradual decline consistent with mature mine sequencing and extension uncertainty. Capex tapered after Year1 as expansion component moderates. 85% attributable interest. Currency: USD.

assets.name: Westwood
assets.notes: 2026 guidance midpoint 120 koz and AISC midpoint $2,025/oz. Years 2-5 modeled as non-flat around guidance midpoint to reflect underground sequencing variability. Capex moderates after Year1 from elevated development spend. 100% interest. Currency: USD.
